We are given \(\mathbf{a} = (1,1,1)^T\), \(\mathbf{a} \cdot \mathbf{v} = 2\), and \(\mathbf{a} \times \mathbf{v} = (1,-2,1)^T\).  
Let \(\mathbf{v} = (x,y,z)^T\). The dot product gives \(x+y+z = 2\).  
The cross product gives:  
\[
\begin{cases}
y - z = 1 \\
z - x = -2 \\
x - y = 1
\end{cases}
\]  
Solving, from \(y - z = 1\) and \(x - y = 1\) we get \(x = y+1\), \(z = y-1\). Substituting into \(x+y+z=2\):
